scenarios.title: NATS client passes auth token to nats.connect when _AUTH_TOKEN is non-empty

gherkin.given: the canonical fail-loud-read constant `app.nats_client._AUTH_TOKEN` is patched to "secret-token"
gherkin.then: `nats.connect` is called exactly once with kwargs that include `token="secret-token"`, and the kwargs do NOT contain any other auth-related parameter, and the kwargs include `servers=["nats://localhost:4222"]` and `name="smackerel-ml"`

scenarios.notes: Adversarial behavioural assertion — would FAIL if a future regression removed the `if _AUTH_TOKEN: connect_opts["token"] = _AUTH_TOKEN` block from `ml/app/nats_client.py`. Patch path `app.nats_client._AUTH_TOKEN` ONLY exists if the canonical constant is imported via `from .auth import _AUTH_TOKEN` at the top of `nats_client.py` — so this test also implicitly verifies the import is present.

scenarios.title: NATS client omits auth token kwarg when _AUTH_TOKEN is the empty-string dev-mode signal

gherkin.given: the canonical fail-loud-read constant `app.nats_client._AUTH_TOKEN` is patched to ""
gherkin.then: `nats.connect` is called exactly once with kwargs that do NOT contain a `token=` key, and the dev-mode auth bypass semantics are preserved (NATS server accepts the connection without auth)

scenarios.notes: Adversarial behavioural assertion — would FAIL if a future regression always set `connect_opts["token"]` regardless of `_AUTH_TOKEN` truthiness (e.g., `connect_opts["token"] = _AUTH_TOKEN or ""`). Preserves the dev-mode auth-bypass semantics established by the pre-fix block.

scenarios.title: ml/app/nats_client.py contains no FORBIDDEN silent-default read of SMACKEREL_AUTH_TOKEN

gherkin.given: the working tree has the BUG-020-004 fix applied
gherkin.then: exit code is 1 (no matches found), and the file imports `_AUTH_TOKEN` from `.auth` at the top of the module, and the file uses `if _AUTH_TOKEN:` followed by `connect_opts["token"] = _AUTH_TOKEN` at exactly one call site inside `NATSClient.connect()`

scenarios.notes: Grep-contract regression test — would FAIL if a future regression re-introduced `os.environ.get("SMACKEREL_AUTH_TOKEN", ...)` or `os.getenv("SMACKEREL_AUTH_TOKEN", ...)` anywhere in `ml/app/nats_client.py`. Test-class location decided by `design.md` DD-9 (Open Question Q-1 resolution): in-file `TestSecretReadContract` class in `ml/tests/test_nats_client.py` (NOT a shared `ml/tests/test_sst_audit.py` module). Per-file scoping wins because the test asserts substring absence in exactly one source file; co-location with the behavioural tests for the same source file aids maintainability. Plan-phase reconciliation by `bubbles.plan` 2026-05-15 keeps `linkedTests[0].testId` on the FROZEN `TestSecretReadContract::test_no_environ_get_smackerel_auth_token_in_nats_client_source` identifier, matching the current implementation state supplied for this repair.
